paragraphs.body: Wrestling is more than just a battle on the mat, it is a powerful teacher that shapes character and instills resilience. You might be asking yourself why he is writing an essay about wrestling. Well for me wrestling is the reason I am here today.

paragraphs.body: Recently I read an article by Kase Nipe titled “How Wrestling Prepares You for Life” in this article he demonstrates and shows us the mindset that wrestlers have and how they got that mindset is through wrestling. But the one thing that I was really fascinated about what he said was “Wrestling isn’t all about bulging biceps and colorful costumes – above all else, it is a tool for personal growth that should not be underestimated” (Nipe). The reason that I like this quote is because without my personal growth and this sport I would not have gotten thought what I had to deal with my senior year of high school.

annotations.comment: Try the summary templates/writing moves we've worked on in class! Also remember that summary is all "they say" without "I say."

paragraphs.body: It was my Senior year, and wrestling season was coming up. I was training and just living life, but one day after practice I got a call from my mom saying my grandfather aka papa was in the hospital with liver cancer. That was just a shock for me because he raised me when my mom and my dad were going thought a divorce. He taught me everything that I know today and when I found out a day after his diagnosis that it was stage 3 and there really nothing, they could do I was devastated. I went to school the next morning acting like everything was fine but when I got to my English class, I had to do an assignment on joe rogan. At this time, I knew who he was and what he did but I did not personally know him. When I was researching him, I found out that he loved wrestling, and he does mix martial arts every day. So, I decided to listen to one of his podcasts and during that podcast he talked about how wrestling is all about discipline and if you have disciplined your set for life. After listening to that, it really got me thinking about my grandpa because he always told me to have discipline, so I was just sad for the rest of the day.

paragraphs.body: But it was time for practice so i went to get ready but during the practice I just was not feeling right. Just me thinking about my grandpa made me not perform good. After practice I got home and i really thought to myself like i can't let this affect my season because i'm trying to go to college for this like I must do good, so I look up more videos for joe rogan and I found this one video on TikTok that he said “is that only true and mentally strong people went through hell”(Rogan). The reason that I like this so much is that he is trying to say tough times create hard men. After hearing that it made me motivated to go all out for my senior season for my grandpa. Thats what I did. I got first in almost every tournament, and I was ranked #3 in all of CA. Thats why I'm so confident that wrestling is the reason that I'm here because even when times were tuff as a wrestler, we deal with that all the time because when you are down in a match and the time is running out your brain tends to not try as hard because you already know the outcome. But if you can push past that point, that's where champions are made and that is where I decided that how I am going to live my life 24/7 because I want to be a NCAA champion and I want to make my grandpa proud in heaven. I just cannot image how proud he will be already of me for making it to college on a scholarship because one of his dreams was to see me wrestle on TV. That is why I believe everyone should give wrestling a shot, even just once. It is not just about physical strength, it is a transformative experience that teaches you about the power of your mind. Wrestling challenges you to confront your limits, develop resilience, and embrace discipline. You will learn to strategize, adapt under pressure, and appreciate the value of challenging work. More than just a sport, wrestling cultivates life skills that can profoundly impact your personal and professional journey.

paragraphs.body: Wrestling is a powerful journey that goes far beyond physical competition. By stepping onto the mat, individuals can unlock invaluable life lessons, such as resilience, discipline, and strategic thinking. For example, even though I had my grandpa pass away I used it as fuel to get me to the next level because I knew he would not want me to just sit around and be devastated about him dying. So, embrace the challenge, who knows what you might learn about yourself along the way!
